highlights.text: Read HEARTBEAT.md if it exists (workspace context). Follow it strictly. Do not infer or repeat old tasks from prior chats. If nothing needs attention, reply HEARTBEAT_OK. When reading HEARTBEAT.md, use workspace file /Users/jl/.openclaw/workspace/HEARTBEAT.md (exact case). Do not read docs/heartbeat.md. Current time: Thursday, March 26th, 2026 — 4:17 AM (America/Los_Angeles) / 2026-03-26 11:17 UTC
Sender (untrusted metadata): ```json { "label": "openclaw-control-ui", "id": "openclaw-control-ui" } ``` [Thu 2026-03-26 07:36 PDT] huh. had no idea each chat thread had a history and could be lost

[[reply_to_current]] Yep — I set up a sane version of that. What I added: - `CHAT_MEMORY_SYSTEM.md` — the operating rules - `memory/chat-index.md` — the “cheat sheet” / index of important conversations - `scripts/session_memory.py` — searches historical OpenClaw session transcripts - updates to `AGENTS.md`, `HEARTBEAT.md`, and `MEMORY.md` so future-me is explicitly told to use this system How it works now: - **Durable summaries** go into `memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md` - **Stable long-term facts** go into `MEMORY.md` - **Important chats/workstreams** get a compact entry in `memory/chat-index.md` - **Old transcripts** remain searchable from `~/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions/*.jsonl` So the workflow becomes: 1. Check `MEMORY.md` 2. Check today/yesterday memory 3. Check `memory/chat-index.md` 4. If context is still fuzzy, search the actual historical transcripts Example commands: - `python3 scripts/session_memory.py recent --days 7` - `python3 scripts/session_memory.py find "still mode notarization"` - `python3 scripts/session_memory.py find "paper trader season 4"` I also logged today’s decision in `memory/2026-03-26.md` and committed the workspace changes. Commit: - `23e51d6` — **Add chat memory indexing and session archive workflow** One caveat: - the search script is currently **keyword-based and lightweight**, which is good for speed and cost - if you want, next step I can make it better by adding: - automatic transcript-to-index summarization - tags/topics extracted from sessions - a “resume project X” helper that searches logs + memory together - maybe a cron/heartbeat-assisted rollup process so this stays maintained automatically If you want, I can do **phase 2** now and make the index more automatic instead of just procedural.

Sender (untrusted metadata): ```json { "label": "openclaw-control-ui", "id": "openclaw-control-ui" } ``` [Thu 2026-03-26 07:42 PDT] yeah, need automatic summarization, tags/topics, the project helper for sure, checking memory (use one of the free models like xai for this), and definitely regular cron/heartbeat to make sure we don't lose anything with longer sessions coming up to limits.

I’m going to extend the system in three layers: docs/process, actual automation scripts for summarization + project lookup, and a scheduled cron reminder that nudges the main session to roll summaries/indexes during the day. I’ll keep the expensive part optional and route the historical-context helper toward free/searchable paths first.
